instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
Can Grandfather Gift an Insurance Policy to a Grandchild in India?
Keywords: insurance for grandchildren, life insurance for children
Meta Description: As a grandparent, you may wish to secure the financial future of your grandchildren with an insurance policy. However, there are certain key pointers you have to keep in mind when doing so. Read on to find out more about these pointers.
Becoming a grandparent is one of the proudest moments of one’s life. While you might want to shower your grandchildren with all the gifts in the world, opting for something that will stand the test of time may be more fruitful. You should consider life insurance for children as a prudent gifting option since such a policy helps get started on securing the financial future of your grandchildren quite early on in life.
Insuring Your Grandchild
As a grandparent, you can buy an insurance policy for your grandchild, after obtaining consent from the child’s parents. Policies with direct benefits like child education plans are more well-suited in such cases as they help save up funds for your grandchild’s future educational expenses. Alternatively, you can also name your grandchildren as beneficiaries to your own whole life plan. In such cases, your grandchildren will be entitled to the death benefit payout from your policy after your passing.
Thus, apart from your love and blessings, a life insurance policy for grandchildren is the next best thing you can give them. However, before you can proceed to sift through plans, it is important to keep certain key pointers in mind. In this article, we cover these key pointers and look at the benefits of opting for such insurance plans for grandchildren.
Things to Know Before Buying Insurance For A Grandchild in India
If you’ve been recently blessed with a grandchild and want to secure her/his future with a life insurance plan for children, here are some of the most important things to keep in mind when picking such a plan:
1.       Demonstrate Insurable Interest
When buying an insurance plan for your grandchild, you first have to demonstrate insurable interest. This basically means that you need to show your insurance provider the cause for insuring a minor. For instance, you can either show your insurer that the policy will go towards financing the education of the child or that you are the sole guardian and primary caretaker of the child. The bottom line is that your insurer has to know why you would want to insure the child.
2.       Eligibility Criteria
The eligibility criteria of a life insurance plan for children vary from one insurance provider to the next. While the tenure of the policy will depend on the type of plan you pick, most child plans cover individuals from the age group of 0 to 17 years.
For instance, a whole life insurance plan for grandchildren can be bought at birth or at a later point in time. The child takes over the premium payments for such a plan at a certain age. However, for certain savings and education plans, the entry age of the child may be set at 18 years by insurance providers.
3.       Type of Plan
While there are various types of child insurance plans available in the market, you should always pick one that best suits your long-term goals and insurance requirements. For instance, you can opt for child ULIP plans that provide both a wealth creation opportunity and life insurance cover for your grandchild. In such a plan, you can either opt for debt-oriented funds or equity funds, as per your risk appetite.
Alternatively, as a grandparent, you can also opt for endowment insurance plans for your grandchildren. Such plans come with a life cover and a savings component. Both ULIP and child endowment plans offer tax benefits under section 80C of the Income Tax Act of 1961.
4.       Determining the Sum Assured Value and Ensuring Affordability
Your investment goals will determine the sum assured for the policy you purchase for your grandchild. For instance, if the policy payout will be used for the child’s higher education funding, your sum assured has to match this goal potential.
Additionally, when purchasing a life insurance plan for grandchildren, you need to take the affordability of the plan into consideration. Since the premiums for the policy will be dependent on the sum assured, the health of the child and the type of policy you pick, you must ensure that you opt for one that comes with affordable premiums and is economically sustainable in the long run.
5.   Policy Tenure and Medical Underwriting
The specific tenure of your child insurance plan will depend on the type of policy you’ve picked. Since different policies cater to different life goals, the tenure for each will also be different.
For instance, if you opt for a whole life insurance plan, it comes with a 99-year tenure. However, policies for newborns mature by the time the individual turns 25-30. Plans that ensure long-term savings and higher education fund reserves for children have higher maturity tenures of 35-45 years.
Additionally, since children are considered low-risk subjects, they generally do not require medical underwriting before being insured. However, if your grandchild happens to suffer from a medical condition, you must disclose the details of the same before purchasing the policy.
Why Should A Grandfather Buy Insurance For His Grandchild?
As a grandfather, by buying a life insurance policy for grandchildren, you are ensuring their long-term financial security. By insuring them at an early age, you seal their insurability that may decline with age. Moreover, with life insurance policies that have a savings component, your grandchildren get to enjoy both assured maturity and death benefits as well as cash benefits that can fund their educational aspirations right up to the university level.
Additionally, if you choose to nominate your grandchildren as beneficiaries under your whole life insurance plan, the death benefits they receive after you pass will be exempted from tax deductions under 10 (10D) of the Income Tax Act, 1961.
Summing It Up
As a grandparent, you would want only the best for your grandchild. Thus, investing in securing their financial future with a life insurance plan seems to be quite prudent. However, before you venture into shortlisting plans, it is important to thoroughly assess your insurance goals. This will help you get a clearer idea of the kind of policy you wish to purchase, its tenure and the sum assured you’re guaranteed under it. However, irrespective of the type of policy you pick, investing in the future of your grandchildren will help give them a head start when pursuing their personal, educational and professional goals.
